"I wanted to reach out as I have recently joined Windsor Path as a Vice President on the M&A team. I'll be supporting new partnerships, and we wanted to connect with folks like yourself given how helpful you are in establishing a pipeline of opportunities. I come from a team that really came to understand how much value a great buy-side deal partner can provide. 

A little about us - we are looking to partner with accounting firms from $3-35m in revenue across the US. Our model is a long-term hold (10+ years), where sellers maintain local equity and autonomy to run their businesses as they see fit, while we provide capital and centralized resources to support growth. We are primarily looking for partners that are planning to stay on for 5 years and are excited to grow the business. We're backed by Peterson Partners and Reef Pass Investors and come from backgrounds in private equity and start-up operations.

Let me know if you have some time to connect in the coming weeks. It would be great to learn if there's an opportunity to work together."

"We are an investment banking firm based in India, specializing in corporate finance advisory services for mid-sized and large corporations. We are currently expanding our operations to the UAE region and are seeking suitable M&A deal opportunities within that market. Our minimum deal ticket size ranges from $10 million to $100 million USD."

"1. Core Financial Metrics

•
EBITDA: AUD $1 million to $3 million.

•
Revenue Model: Strong preference for B2B models with a high percentage of recurring or re-occurring revenue.

2. Geographic Focus

•
Primary Region: Sydney, NSW, and the surrounding 300km radius.

3. Industry & Business Model Focus

In-Scope:

•
AI Enhancement, Not Disruption: Businesses in sectors that can be enhanced and scaled through the application of AI, rather than being disrupted by it.

•
B2B Services & Niche Manufacturing: Businesses with a strong B2B focus, defensible market position, and established client relationships.

Exclusions (Out-of-Scope):

•
Retail: No brick-and-mortar or direct-to-consumer retail.

•
Franchises: No franchise models.

•
Hospitality: No restaurants, cafes, bars, or hotels.

•
E-commerce Vulnerability: No businesses selling products that are easily freighted and susceptible to disruption from platforms like Amazon or Temu.

4. Ownership & Succession Profile (High-Priority Signals)

•
Founder Demographics: Actively seeking businesses where the founder or key owner is over the age of 65, indicating potential succession planning needs.

•
Family Dynamics & Real Estate Motivation: High interest in scenarios where the business occupies valuable real estate and the next generation is not interested in running the company but wishes to retain the property and cash.

•
Specific Signal: Search for key family members who have departed the business within the last 5 years.



•
Past Sale History: Identify businesses that have been listed for sale previously but did not sell. This may indicate a motivated seller or a valuation gap we can bridge.

5. Strategic Acquisition Opportunities

•
Competitor Acquisition: Identify businesses that are direct competitors to a company currently for sale. The competitor may represent a better, off-market acquisition opportunity that could be approached directly."

"Hi!

I’m Lawrence Mortenson, CEO at TESMO, an e-commerce/marketplace services platform. We’re preparing our first acquisition (or add-on) across smaller service firms (Amazon/marketplace + brand support agencies) and want to build a short, high-fit target list of ~30 companies that meet the following criteria:

Target profile:

EBITDA in the ~$150K - $300k range

Revenue ~ ~$600K-$1.2M (or consistent with above EBITDA)

Service clients in Amazon, Walmart.com, e-commerce brand support space (creative, ad tech, marketplace operations)

Founder-operated or small owner-operator, ideally open to joining a platform post-deal

US-based or North America, remote-friendly operations

Deliverable required:

List of ~30 companies with company name, estimated revenue/EBITDA, founder/owner name, location, service description, approximate client type, and founder contact info (email + phone if available)

Prioritization/ranking by “fit” score (1-10) with comments on integration or seller openness if available

Timeline: initial list delivered 4-6 weeks from now

We’re looking to engage a partner for this list build and would like to understand your:

Methodology for sourcing and verifying company data

Pricing model (flat fee vs per-company vs subscription)

Turnaround time

Sample deliverable or previous case studies for similar size deals

Please let me know your availability for a 20-minute call this week to discuss further.

Regards,

Lawrence

"Strategic bolt-on acquisition based on exclusivity matching the current alt-data market boom.  Invitezy — Strategic Signal Infrastructure for Institutional Alpha
Invitezy is a defensible, first-party signal infrastructure platform engineered for institutional buyers seeking predictive intelligence across global public and private markets.
Built at the nexus of global operational flow, Invitezy transforms timestamped hiring behavior into allocator-grade signals — mapped to public tickers, enriched with behavioral overlays, and structured for hedge-fund and PE ingestion.
The platform captures real-time global operational activity, hiring intent, outsourcing trends, and workforce engagement across enterprises worldwide. Scalable and operational, Invitezy currently focuses on the India corridor — one of the world’s richest operational signal zones and a leading indicator of margin movement, product scaling, and geographic expansion.

 Institutional Validation & Tier-1 Traction
Invitezy has already attracted meaningful engagement from leading institutional data buyers:
Squarepoint Capital — pilot access requested


Bloomberg — under review for private-market expansion


Citadel — internal evaluation underway


Capital Fund Management (CFM) — inbound information request


Neudata — formally indexed and validated for alt-data workflows


This traction validates Invitezy as a differentiated, ingestible signal layer for both quantitative and discretionary investment strategies.
INvitezy pre-inflection exploring strategic acquisition of proprietary data pipeline. Looking for strategic fit with company that has access strategic acquirers based on sale of IP"

"I have a rare opportunity. It’s a product. A “liquid nicotine cigarette” called Smoke-Break.

It checks many boxes: 

(1) It has scarcity
(2) It’s IP includes an approved IND from the FDA, a published clinical study, 3 US Patents, a trademark, and liquidcigarette.com
(3) Smoke-Break is now dormant, and easily acquirable, possibly for free. I know how. 
(4) It’ll have a lot of friends, but no enemies
(5) For minimal upfront costs (<$100,000 US), this project can be turned into a highly-desired acquisition target for someone… within two (2) years. 

I’m the inventor of Smoke-Break and founder of the company. We’re like a “safe alternative to the electronic cigarette.”

Smoke-Break is presently inactive, and discarded. I own 8% of Smoke-Break, which is down from 100% in the old days. But I’m excited. The rise of the e-cigarette makes this a gold mine for someone.

I made a brief video (less than 5 min) which gives the history of Smoke-Break and its current opportunity.

Brett Roth"

"I’m Darren, founder of Invitezy — a global alt-data signal infrastructure platform built for institutional workflows across both public and private markets.

We’re already seeing strong traction:
	•	Squarepoint piloting our ESG drift & infra fragility overlays
	•	Citadel evaluating LP liquidity stress signals
	•	Bloomberg in exploratory ingestion conversations
	•	Neudata-listed, giving us institutional validation and discovery

What makes Invitezy unique:
	•	Dual-market architecture – signals mapped to public tickers and private entities
	•	Macro corridors – ESG drift, infra fragility, LP stress, thematic overlays
	•	Allocator-grade packaging – ingestion-ready formats for funds and allocators
	•	Exclusivity – founder-controlled, limited access for defensibility

Given SourceCo’s strength in connecting data and analytics businesses with strategic buyers, I’d love to explore whether Invitezy might be a fit for your network. Our exclusivity position, coupled with Tier-1 validation, makes this a scarce asset at a time when strategics (S&P, FactSet, Nasdaq, Preqin, Datasite) are actively consolidating alt-data.

We’re looking to move quickly and would be open to structuring a success-fee-weighted engagement, given the inbound demand already on the table.
